content.problemStatement:
Write a program to calculate the total marks obtained in three subjects where the marks may include decimal values (`85.5`, `90.0` & `88.3`). Use the addition (`+`) operator and print the total marks with one decimal place.

**Hint**: Use %.1f to print a float value with 1 digit after the decimal point. (We will learn more about such formatting in the float topic.)
constraints: Each mark is given with at most one decimal place
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of three lines, each containing a decimal number (0.0 to 100.0) with at most one digit after the decimal point.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a line in the format: Total Marks = X where X is the sum of the three marks printed with exactly one digit after the decimal point.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The three marks are 85.5, 90.0 and 88.3. Their sum is 263.8, printed with one decimal place.
Adding the three equal marks 50.0 each gives 150.0.

hidden.label: stress/general
hidden.expectedOutput:
Total Marks = 263.8
Total Marks = 100.0
Total Marks = 70.0

solution.approach: For every test case we simply read the three floating‑point marks, add them using double precision and print the result with one digit after the decimal point. The addition is O(1) per test case and the formatting %.1f (or String.format) guarantees the required output format.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – we perform a constant amount of work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used irrespective of T.

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();for(int t=0;t<T;t++){double m1=Double.parseDouble(br.readLine().trim());double m2=Double.parseDouble(br.readLine().trim());double m3=Double.parseDouble(br.readLine().trim());double total=m1+m2+m3;sb.append(String.format("Total Marks = %.1f",total));if(t!=T-1)sb.append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
